title: ATM Second Brain — Note Frontmatter Contract v1
description: Canonical, machine-readable contract for the YAML frontmatter of every note in the vault. Validation is hand-rolled in the stdlib MCP server (no jsonschema dependency); this file is the single source of truth that both the server and the human read from. Append-only and immutable once published: changes ship as schema.vN, never edits to v1.

schema_version.description: Integer schema version this note is written against. Writes MUST equal the integer in _schema/CURRENT; the server never auto-upgrades. Migration is the only path to a higher version.

id.description: Stable identity, never reused: UTC timestamp YYYYMMDDhhmmss + '-' + kebab slug. Links resolve by id/alias, never by filename, so notes can be renamed freely.

title.description: Human-facing title. May change; identity lives in id.

type.description: Note role. Drives folder placement convention and review behavior.

created.description: UTC creation date (YYYY-MM-DD). Immutable after first write.

updated.description: UTC date of last content change (YYYY-MM-DD).

trust_tier.description: Provenance trust level. Fail-closed: unknown or missing => treated as externally-ingested. Subject to a monotonic anti-laundering floor — a note's effective tier can never silently rise.

author.description: Who produced the prose. 'human' atoms are immutable to the agent. 'mixed' requires span-level provenance in the body or provenance block.

domain.description: Optional life/expertise domain tag (e.g. 'web-design', 'business', 'image-video', 'counsel'). Used to route expertise packs.

tags.description: Lowercase kebab tags; '/' allowed for hierarchy. The tag graph is built from these, separate from the FTS index.

aliases.description: Alternate names a wikilink may resolve to. Add an alias instead of renaming to preserve inbound links.

sources.description: Citations backing claims in this note. citation_verify checks every entry resolves.

locator.description: Page / section / timestamp within the source.

provenance.description: Machine-managed provenance block. Written by write_with_provenance; the tier_lineage is append-only.

source.description: Where the content originated (human, agent-run id, external ingest id).

content_hash.description: sha256 of the note body at write time; powers the integrity manifest and idempotent reindex.

tier_lineage.description: Append-only history of trust_tier transitions; the anti-laundering floor is the max-trust ever justified, not the latest claim.
